nodes.label: Sent Tech Design System
nodes.rationale: Shared Svelte design system for Sent Tech products with white-label theming, runtime tenant support, build-time CSS export, and bilingual documentation.

nodes.label: @sent-tech/tokens
nodes.rationale: Foundation, semantic, and component token source of truth.

nodes.label: @sent-tech/themes
nodes.rationale: Theme package for Sent Tech, Forge, Entropic, and white-label outputs.

nodes.label: @sent-tech/components-svelte
nodes.rationale: Svelte components styled through Sent Tech tokens.

nodes.label: Carbon-like bilingual docs app
nodes.rationale: Interactive documentation inspired by Carbon, available in French and English.

nodes.label: Public Repository Safety
nodes.rationale: The repository is meant to be public, so secrets, proprietary excerpts, private URLs, and unvalidated client details are excluded.

nodes.label: Token-first architecture
nodes.rationale: The plan starts from a shared token foundation before components, so products can converge without losing distinct branding.

nodes.label: Foundation semantic component tokens
nodes.rationale: The target taxonomy separates raw values, semantic roles, and component-specific refinements.

nodes.label: Make or buy strategy
nodes.rationale: The plan evaluates custom tokens/themes plus possible external accessible primitives, while keeping product identity and business components custom.

nodes.label: sentech-forge needs
nodes.rationale: Forge provides marketing/editorial surfaces, semantic Tailwind v4 tokens, navigation, hero, services, blog, and CTA patterns.

nodes.label: top-ai app needs
nodes.rationale: The app needs dense enterprise primitives: forms, tables, overlays, chat, collaboration, data visualization, reports, extension and webview compatibility.

nodes.label: Shared primitives backlog
nodes.rationale: The implementation lot lists buttons, links, inputs, cards, badges, alerts, menu/popover/dropdown, dialog/drawer, tabs, table, toast, empty state and loading state.

nodes.label: White-label runtime theming
nodes.rationale: Validated decision: white-label support must work at tenant runtime with optional build-time export.

nodes.label: External design-system adapters
nodes.rationale: The design system keeps a Sent Tech core and allows compatibility adapters for Airbus-like external systems.

nodes.label: Chat UI incubation
nodes.rationale: Chat UI components are intentionally not frozen until Entropic finishes refactoring.

nodes.label: Design system quality gate
nodes.rationale: Each stable component needs documentation, examples, token references, main states, accessibility checks, tests, and package build validation.

nodes.label: Entropic chat refactor dependency
nodes.rationale: The DS provides stable chat tokens and runtime contracts now, but component APIs wait for Entropic to expose a settled API.

nodes.label: Chat shell modes
nodes.rationale: Supported modes are floating, docked, sidepanel, and embedded surfaces.

nodes.label: Chat runtime events
nodes.rationale: The contract names streaming message deltas, tool lifecycle, permission requests, and checkpoint requests.

nodes.label: Chat token groups
nodes.rationale: Chat surfaces consume component chat tokens and semantic status tokens for pending, processing, completed, and failed states.

nodes.label: Forge low-coupling integration
nodes.rationale: First Forge integration validates Sent Tech tokens without replacing Forge components.

nodes.label: Forge vendor-copy CSS
nodes.rationale: Recommended first Forge commit copies the generated Forge CSS theme into Forge source instead of depending on npm packages immediately.

nodes.label: Forge theme scope attribute
nodes.rationale: Forge should mark its root with data-st-theme="forge" so theme variables are scoped predictably.

nodes.label: Forge CSS compatibility bridge
nodes.rationale: Existing Forge styles should map only obvious surface, text, action, border and focus variables to Sent Tech tokens.

nodes.label: Forge component pilot
nodes.rationale: After the CSS-only pass, one Forge screen should replace a small set of primitives with @sent-tech/components-svelte.

nodes.label: Forge relay prompt
nodes.rationale: Operational prompt for applying the low-coupling Forge integration in the Forge repository.

nodes.label: npm Trusted Publishing release
nodes.rationale: Public npm packages are released through tag-driven GitHub Actions and npm OIDC Trusted Publishing.

nodes.label: Pack smoke test
nodes.rationale: Pre-release checks pack the three package tarballs and install them together in a clean temporary project.

nodes.label: Release guard workflow
nodes.rationale: The workflow refuses to publish tags not merged into the default branch and verifies package versions match the tag.

nodes.label: Post-publish npm check
nodes.rationale: The workflow waits for npm propagation then installs the published package versions to verify imports.

nodes.label: Forms component slice
nodes.rationale: Slice A adds Input, Textarea, Select, Checkbox, Radio, and Switch as stable Svelte 5 primitives.

nodes.label: Overlay and feedback slice
nodes.rationale: The Form + Overlay report records Modal, Tooltip, Toast and related token groups as completed.

nodes.label: Data navigation slice
nodes.rationale: The data/navigation slice covers Table, Tabs, Pagination, Breadcrumb and SideNav with data-driven APIs.

nodes.label: Component API principles
nodes.rationale: Components pass through native HTML attributes, expose explicit state props only when needed, use st-prefixed classes, and avoid product-specific behavior.

nodes.label: Component TDD verification
nodes.rationale: Component groups are implemented with failing tests first, svelte-check, package build, docs build, and root verify before final status.

nodes.label: Foundation V1 verified
nodes.rationale: Foundation V1 report marks monorepo, tokens, themes, Svelte components, bilingual docs, Chat UI contract and global verification as complete.
